fixture.description: New transfer method nests synchronized(from){synchronized(to){}} so two threads calling transfer(a,b) and transfer(b,a) acquire the two monitors in opposite order — a classic AB-BA deadlock.

grader.rubric: PASS if the report identifies the lock-ordering (AB-BA) deadlock: two concurrent calls transfer(a, b) and transfer(b, a) acquire the `from` and `to` monitors in opposite orders and can deadlock; the fix is to impose a global lock ordering (e.g. lock the lower account id first). Acceptable severity: CRITICAL or IMPORTANT. FAIL if the deadlock risk is not surfaced. The report should not reduce this to a single-lock or reentrancy issue.

expected_behavior: Concurrency/bugs agent flags the AB-BA deadlock and recommends a stable global lock order
expected_not_behavior: Does NOT claim a single-lock reentrancy problem instead of the ordering deadlock
